role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; his testimony clusters with the Mississippi voter-registration cohort and lands at a near-maximum Collective Discipline reading on the corpus's individual-vs-collective axis.
ai_reading: Conway's top semantic neighbors are Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 76), Barbara Edna Vickers (entry 5), Gwendolyn Annette Perkins Duncan (entry 51), Frankye Adams-Johnson (entry 43), and Robert Bagner Hayling (entry 103, St. Augustine SCLC). The cluster bridges Mississippi voter-registration (McLaurin, Adams-Johnson) and Florida SCLC (Hayling). The catalog's strongest observation about him is on the Individual Conscience vs Collective Discipline axis: he sits at +0.82, very near the Collective Discipline pole and matching the Mary Jenkins (entry 84, +0.83) reading. His four other axis positions cluster near zero (range +0.07 to +0.17), so the collective-discipline pull is isolated, an unusual structural pattern that may catch institutional or organizational testimony content the cultural reading of him would not have predicted.
biographical_paragraph: Purcell Maurice Conway's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 99 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
